summary: Comprehensive product-ideal blueprint for the manager-host runtime boundary covering bootstrap, supervision, gateway, dashboard recovery, and workspace persistence. Strong self-description via defines/means blocks and clear causal chains. Abstraction discipline is mixed: some rules encode implementation-level specifics (hardcoded filesystem paths, specific recovery step sequences) that belong in an implementation-resolved layer rather than the product ideal.
strengths: Excellent self-description: role declaration, defines block, and means blocks allow full recovery without companion context
Rich causal topology: when/then chains for dashboard session issuance, gateway lazy-start, and operator update workflows encode real system behavior with clear triggers and consequences
Thorough policy separation: source-of-truth, script-boundary, logging, and full-update-workflow policies are individually named, defined, and enforced
Good semantic density in enforces and prescribes blocks: each rule carries distinct operational meaning with minimal redundancy
Strong corpus usefulness: an agent encountering this file gains a complete mental model of the runtime boundary contract
weaknesses: Abstraction discipline leak: hardcoded paths like /home/ec2-user/workspace/README.md and /home/ec2-user/state/logs/system-events.log push implementation-resolved detail into a product-ideal file
Dashboard recovery when/then chain is excessively long (15+ chained .and clauses) which reduces scanability and makes individual rules harder to locate or revise independently
Some entity declarations (e.g., RuntimeCode group with 11 members) are defined but only lightly connected in the topology, creating declaration weight without proportional causal payoff

The fullUpdateWorkflow means block mixes product-level intent (verify locally, commit, push) with agent-instruction-level directives (use the browser tool, capture screenshots) that belong in a process guide rather than a product ideal
